summary: Iteration 24 — Destructive action confirmation token gate (remove_allocation, delete_wbs_task) and manage_phases merge mode. 23/23 new tests PASS. 51/51 regression tests (iter19-22) PASS. Total: 74/74 green.
critical_code_review_comments: SIDE EFFECT during initial test run: admin@test.com has role=super_admin (promoted in iter19) so the first (broken) test inadvertently deleted the seeded 'Legacy System' project — super_admin bypasses confirmation. Restored manually. E1 should be aware: any test that exercises destructive actions via HTTP must use a non-super_admin credential.

test_credentials.md note: admin@test.com is now super_admin in this DB, so it cannot be used as a regular admin for confirmation-gate tests. The iteration 24 test suite creates a temporary testadmin_iter24@test.com (role=admin) for this purpose and tears it down after the run.

The _consume_confirmation_token helper in ai_action_registry.py has a subtle timezone comparison: `exp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) if exp.tzinfo is None else exp`. The outer if-condition `if exp and ...` is always truthy (when exp is not None), which means it always runs the inner `if datetime.now(timezone.utc) > ...`. This logic is correct but slightly convoluted — a clean rewrite would help readability.

Positive: All 5 test groups passed cleanly — (1) remove_allocation confirmation, (2) delete_wbs_task confirmation, (3) manage_phases merge/replace/add, (4) build_actions_prompt destructive flags, (5) regression non-destructive immediate execution.

test_credentials.temp_admin_created_by_tests: testadmin_iter24@test.com / TestAdmin123! (created in setup_module, deleted in teardown_module)
test_credentials.note: admin@test.com = super_admin in this DB — cannot use for confirmation gate tests

seed_data_creation: Restored 'Legacy System' project (deleted accidentally by first test run using super_admin token). All other test data (allocations, WBS tasks, projects, temp admin user) created and cleaned up by the test harness.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 74/74 tests pass (23 new + 51 regression). Key test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration24_destructive_confirmation.py. Uses a module-level persistent asyncio event loop (_LOOP) to work around Motor's event-loop-closed issue when asyncio.run() is called multiple times. Creates temp admin user testadmin_iter24@test.com (role=admin) for HTTP confirmation gate tests and tears it down. The 'Legacy System' project was accidentally deleted during a failed test run and has been manually restored. All destructive action confirmation logic and manage_phases merge mode verified as working correctly.
rca_of_side_effect: Root cause: admin@test.com was promoted to super_admin during iter19 consistency testing. Initial test version used admin@test.com for HTTP tests — super_admin bypasses confirmation and immediately deleted 'Legacy System'. Fix: created dedicated temp admin (role=admin) user for iteration 24 HTTP confirmation tests. Restoration: inserted Legacy System project directly via motor with same structure as seed data.
